titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University School of Civil and Environmental Engineering records16-4-23

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:08:11 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMA00023Cornell University School of Civil and Environmental Engineering records16-4-23Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:12:30 UTC.Describing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University School of Civil and Environmental Engineering records
Cornell University. College of Civil Engineering
Schurman, Jacob Gould, 1854-1942.
Thurston, Robert Henry, 1839-1903.
Washburn, Frank S. (Frank Sherman), 1860-1922.
Treman, Robert H. (Robert Henry), 1858-1937.
Marx, Charles D., 1857-1939.
Kerr, Walter C., 1858-1910.
16-4-23
8 cubic feet.
3 mapcase folders.
4 volumes.
9.4 megabytes.
1900-2015. In English. 8948820
Scope and content
Correspondence and circular letters surrounding the search for a new Dean or Director for the College of Civil Engineering. Correspondents include Cornell University President Jacob Gould Schurman, Robert H. Thurston, Frank S. Washburn, R. H. Treman, Charles D. Marx, Walter C. Kerr, and many others.
Preferred Citation
Cornell University School of Civil and Environmental Engineering records #16-4-23. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Access
Annual reports are restricted to permission of the office of origin.
Related Materials
For additional material relating to the Cornell University College of Engineering summer surveying camp see collection #16-4-956.
Engineering schools -- New York (State) -- Ithaca -- Administration. Circular letters. Crandall, C. L. (Charles Lee), 1850-1917. Cornell University. College of Civil Engineering -- : Administration. Cornell University -- : Administration.

Includes reports for Department of Structural Engineering
